newsSince: On June 17, 2026 Toromont disclosed that its Power Systems unit (AVL Manufacturing) received confirmation of ~CAD$1 billion of firm orders for power generation/storage enclosures, primarily for data-center applications, with delivery substantially in 2027. The orders require incremental capacity beyond 2026; the company is acquiring land for a new Hamilton, Ontario facility and ramping Charlotte, NC production. This is the first hard data-center demand evidence behind the thesis.

sources.label: Toromont Power Systems update (Globe and Mail)

verdicts.rankRationale: June 17 AVL update converts the speculative 'equipment/service supports compute campuses' pathway into ~C$1B of firm data-center enclosure orders (2027 delivery) plus new-facility capex. First tangible AI-chain evidence since June 9; lifts residual latent alpha on this strict screen.
verdicts.bull: ~C$1B firm data-center enclosure backlog + capacity expansion is hard, near-monopoly Cat-dealer-adjacent exposure to compute-campus power buildout.
verdicts.neutral: Real order but delivery mostly 2027; AVL is a minority of the group and stock already at 52-wk high near consensus target.
verdicts.bear: +41% YTD leaves little margin of safety; data-center enclosure demand is cyclical and a single large order can be lumpy.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: ~C$1B AVL data-center enclosure order book, 2027 delivery
